idioms: Piece of cake: Something that is /h1extremely easy/h1 to do or understand; a task that /h2requires little effort/h2. /b1قطعة كعك (سهل جداً)/b1 Example: I thought the exam would be hard, but it was a /c1piece of cake/c1.

Bite the bullet: To endure a /h1painful or otherwise unpleasant/h1 situation that is seen as /h2unavoidable/h2. /b1يتحمل الصعاب / يتجرع المرارة/b1 Example: I hate going to the dentist, but I'll just have to /c1bite the bullet/c1 and go.

Break the ice: To do or say something that makes people feel more /h1comfortable/h1 and helps them start /h2talking/h2 in a social situation. /b1كسر الجليد/b1 Example: He told a joke to /c1break the ice/c1 at the meeting.

Hit the nail on the head: To describe exactly what is /h1causing a problem/h1 or to say something /h2completely correct/h2. /b1أصاب كبد الحقيقة/b1 Example: You /c1hit the nail on the head/c1 when you said the problem was communication.

Let the cat out of the bag: To accidentally /h1reveal a secret/h1 or surprise that was meant to be /h2kept hidden/h2. /b1كشف السر/b1 Example: Don't /c1let the cat out of the bag/c1 about the surprise party!
The ball is in your court: It's /h1your turn/h1 to make a decision or take /h2action/h2. /b1الكرة في ملعبك/b1 Example: I've made my offer, now /c1the ball is in your court/c1.
Once in a blue moon: Something that happens /h1very rarely/h1 or almost /h2never/h2. /b1نادراً جداً/b1 Example: I /c1once in a blue moon/c1 eat fast food.
Under the weather: Feeling /h1slightly ill/h1 or /h2unwell/h2. /b1غير مرتاح/b1 Example: I'm feeling a bit /c1under the weather/c1 today.
Costs an arm and a leg: Something that is /h1very expensive/h1 or costs /h2too much money/h2. /b1غالي جداً/b1 Example: That new phone /c1costs an arm and a leg/c1.
Spill the beans: To /h1reveal secret information/h1 or tell something that was /h2supposed to be kept quiet/h2. /b1يفشي السر/b1 Example: Who /c1spilled the beans/c1 about the surprise?
Beat around the bush: To /h1avoid talking/h1 about what is important or to /h2delay getting to the point/h2. /b1يلف ويدور/b1 Example: Stop /c1beating around the bush/c1 and tell me what happened.
On cloud nine: Feeling /h1extremely happy/h1 or /h2delighted/h2. /b1في قمة السعادة/b1 Example: She was /c1on cloud nine/c1 after getting the job.

A blessing in disguise: Something that seems /h1bad at first/h1 but turns out to be /h2good/h2. /b1نعمة مقنعة/b1 Example: Losing that job was /c1a blessing in disguise/c1 because I found a better one.

Kill two birds with one stone: To accomplish /h1two tasks/h1 with /h2one action/h2. /b1يضرب عصفورين بحجر واحد/b1 Example: By walking to work, I exercise and save money - /c1killing two birds with one stone/c1.
Got cold feet: To suddenly become /h1too nervous/h1 or /h2afraid/h2 to do something you planned. /b1يصاب بالخوف في اللحظة الأخيرة/b1 Example: He /c1got cold feet/c1 before the presentation.

Every cloud has a silver lining: Every /h1bad situation/h1 has /h2some good aspect/h2 to it. /b1لكل محنة منحة/b1 Example: I failed the test, but /c1every cloud has a silver lining/c1 - now I know what to study.
When pigs fly: Something that will /h1never happen/h1 or is /h2completely impossible/h2. /b1مستحيل الحدوث/b1 Example: He'll clean his room /c1when pigs fly/c1.

The best of both worlds: A situation where you can enjoy /h1two different advantages/h1 at the /h2same time/h2. /b1أفضل ما في العالمين/b1 Example: Working from home gives me /c1the best of both worlds/c1 - flexibility and income.
See eye to eye: To /h1agree/h1 with someone or have the /h2same opinion/h2. /b1يتفق في الرأي/b1 Example: We don't always /c1see eye to eye/c1 on politics.
Speak of the devil: Said when someone you were /h1just talking about/h1 /h2appears/h2 unexpectedly. /b1اذكر القط يجيك نط/b1 Example: /c1Speak of the devil/c1 - here comes Ahmad now!
Break a leg: Used to wish someone /h1good luck/h1, especially before a /h2performance or presentation/h2. /b1حظاً سعيداً/b1 Example: /c1Break a leg/c1 on your test today!
It's raining cats and dogs: It's /h1raining very heavily/h1 or /h2pouring/h2. /b1تمطر بغزارة/b1 Example: We can't go outside - it's /c1raining cats and dogs/c1.

Barking up the wrong tree: Looking for solutions in the /h1wrong place/h1 or /h2blaming the wrong person/h2. /b1يتهم الشخص الخطأ/b1 Example: If you think I took your pen, you're /c1barking up the wrong tree/c1.
A dime a dozen: Something that is /h1very common/h1 and has /h2little value/h2. /b1شائع جداً/b1 Example: Good phone apps are /c1a dime a dozen/c1 these days.

Comparing apples to oranges: Comparing two things that are /h1completely different/h1 and /h2cannot be compared/h2. /b1مقارنة غير عادلة/b1 Example: Comparing my school to yours is like /c1comparing apples to oranges/c1.

Don't cry over spilled milk: Don't waste time feeling /h1upset/h1 about something that /h2already happened/h2 and cannot be changed. /b1لا تبكِ على اللبن المسكوب/b1 Example: You failed one test - /c1don't cry over spilled milk/c1 and study harder next time.
Easy as pie: Something that is /h1extremely simple/h1 or /h2requires no effort/h2. /b1سهل جداً/b1 Example: Learning to use this app is /c1easy as pie/c1.

Jump on the bandwagon: To /h1join others/h1 in doing something that is /h2becoming popular/h2. /b1يتبع الموضة/b1 Example: Everyone is learning English, so I decided to /c1jump on the bandwagon/c1.
Keep your chin up: To /h1stay positive/h1 and /h2not give up/h2 during difficult times. /b1ابقَ متفائلاً/b1 Example: /c1Keep your chin up/c1 - things will get better!
Let sleeping dogs lie: To /h1avoid interfering/h1 with a situation that could cause /h2trouble/h2. /b1دع الأمور كما هي/b1 Example: Don't mention the argument - better to /c1let sleeping dogs lie/c1.
Miss the boat: To /h1miss an opportunity/h1 or be /h2too late/h2 for something. /b1يفوت الفرصة/b1 Example: Register now or you'll /c1miss the boat/c1.
No pain, no gain: You can't achieve /h1success/h1 without /h2hard work or effort/h2. /b1لا نجاح بدون تعب/b1 Example: Practice is hard, but /c1no pain, no gain/c1.
Off the hook: To be /h1freed/h1 from /h2responsibility or trouble/h2. /b1تخلص من المسؤولية/b1 Example: My mom said I'm /c1off the hook/c1 for doing dishes tonight.
On the fence: Being /h1uncertain/h1 or /h2unable to decide/h2 between two options. /b1متردد/b1 Example: I'm /c1on the fence/c1 about which subject to choose.
Out of the blue: Something that happens /h1suddenly/h1 and /h2unexpectedly/h2. /b1فجأة/b1 Example: He called me /c1out of the blue/c1 after two years.
Pull yourself together: To /h1calm down/h1 and /h2regain control/h2 of your emotions. /b1اهدأ واتزن/b1 Example: Stop crying and /c1pull yourself together/c1.

The elephant in the room: An /h1obvious problem/h1 that everyone /h2avoids talking about/h2. /b1المشكلة الواضحة المتجاهلة/b1 Example: Let's discuss /c1the elephant in the room/c1 - we're all struggling with this lesson.
Through thick and thin: During /h1good times/h1 and /h2bad times/h2. /b1في السراء والضراء/b1 Example: My friends support me /c1through thick and thin/c1.
Time flies: Time /h1passes/h1 /h2very quickly/h2. /b1الوقت يمر بسرعة/b1 Example: We've been in school for three months already - /c1time flies/c1!
Up in the air: Something that is /h1uncertain/h1 or /h2not yet decided/h2. /b1غير محسوم/b1 Example: Our plans for the weekend are still /c1up in the air/c1.

You can't judge a book by its cover: You shouldn't /h1judge something/h1 based only on its /h2appearance/h2. /b1لا تحكم على الكتاب من غلافه/b1 Example: The movie looked boring, but /c1you can't judge a book by its cover/c1 - it was great!

Actions speak louder than words: What you /h1do/h1 is more important than what you /h2say/h2. /b1الأفعال أبلغ من الأقوال/b1 Example: He promised to help but didn't - /c1actions speak louder than words/c1.

Better late than never: It's better to do something /h1late/h1 than to /h2not do it at all/h2. /b1أن تأتي متأخراً خير من ألا تأتي/b1 Example: You finished your homework at midnight, but /c1better late than never/c1.

Don't put all your eggs in one basket: Don't risk /h1everything/h1 on /h2one plan or option/h2. /b1لا تضع كل البيض في سلة واحدة/b1 Example: Apply to several universities - /c1don't put all your eggs in one basket/c1.

Get a taste of your own medicine: To experience the /h1same bad treatment/h1 that you /h2gave to others/h2. /b1يذوق من نفس الكأس/b1 Example: He was always late, so when I was late he /c1got a taste of his own medicine/c1.
Give someone the cold shoulder: To /h1ignore someone/h1 or treat them in an /h2unfriendly way/h2. /b1يتجاهل شخصاً/b1 Example: She /c1gave me the cold shoulder/c1 after our argument.
Have your head in the clouds: To be /h1unrealistic/h1 or /h2not paying attention/h2. /b1شارد الذهن/b1 Example: Stop /c1having your head in the clouds/c1 and focus on your work.
It takes two to tango: Both people involved in a /h1situation/h1 are /h2responsible/h2 for it. /b1الأمر يحتاج طرفين/b1 Example: Don't blame only him - /c1it takes two to tango/c1.
Keep an eye on: To /h1watch/h1 or /h2monitor something/h2 carefully. /b1راقب/b1 Example: Can you /c1keep an eye on/c1 my bag while I'm gone?
Learn the ropes: To /h1learn/h1 the /h2basic skills/h2 needed for something. /b1يتعلم الأساسيات/b1 Example: It took me a week to /c1learn the ropes/c1 at my new school.
Make a long story short: To /h1summarize/h1 or tell something /h2briefly/h2. /b1باختصار/b1 Example: /c1To make a long story short/c1, we won the game.
Once bitten, twice shy: After a /h1bad experience/h1, you become /h2more careful/h2. /b1من جُرب المجرّب عقله مخرّب/b1 Example: I won't trust him again - /c1once bitten, twice shy/c1.
Practice makes perfect: Doing something /h1repeatedly/h1 makes you /h2better at it/h2. /b1التمرين يصنع الإتقان/b1 Example: Keep studying - /c1practice makes perfect/c1!
Rome wasn't built in a day: Important work takes /h1time/h1 and /h2cannot be rushed/h2. /b1روما لم تُبنَ في يوم/b1 Example: Learning English is hard, but /c1Rome wasn't built in a day/c1.

The early bird catches the worm: People who /h1start early/h1 or /h2arrive first/h2 have an advantage. /b1من يصحو باكراً ينال المراد/b1 Example: I got to school early and got the best seat - /c1the early bird catches the worm/c1.

Two heads are better than one: Working with /h1another person/h1 makes tasks /h2easier/h2. /b1اثنان أفضل من واحد/b1 Example: Let's work together - /c1two heads are better than one/c1.

Where there's a will, there's a way: If you are /h1determined/h1 enough, you can /h2find a solution/h2. /b1حيث توجد إرادة توجد وسيلة/b1 Example: Don't give up - /c1where there's a will, there's a way/c1.
You reap what you sow: You will experience the /h1consequences/h1 of your /h2actions/h2. /b1تحصد ما تزرع/b1 Example: He didn't study and failed - /c1you reap what you sow/c1.

A picture is worth a thousand words: A /h1visual image/h1 can explain something /h2better than words/h2. /b1صورة واحدة بألف كلمة/b1 Example: Look at this diagram - /c1a picture is worth a thousand words/c1.

Down in the dumps: Feeling /h1sad/h1 or /h2depressed/h2. /b1حزين ومكتئب/b1 Example: He's been /c1down in the dumps/c1 since he failed the test.

When it rains, it pours: When /h1bad things/h1 happen, they happen /h2all at once/h2. /b1المصائب لا تأتي فرادى/b1 Example: First I forgot my book, then I failed the quiz - /c1when it rains, it pours/c1.

Come hell or high water: /h1No matter/h1 what /h2difficulties arise/h2. /b1مهما كانت الصعوبات/b1 Example: I'll finish this project /c1come hell or high water/c1.
Cut somebody some slack: To /h1be less critical/h1 or /h2give someone a break/h2. /b1يتسامح مع شخص/b1 Example: /c1Cut me some slack/c1 - I'm doing my best!
Dead as a doornail: Completely /h1dead/h1 or /h2non-functional/h2. /b1ميت تماماً/b1 Example: My phone battery is /c1dead as a doornail/c1.

Don't count your chickens before they hatch: Don't /h1assume success/h1 /h2before it happens/h2. /b1لا تفرح قبل أوانه/b1 Example: /c1Don't count your chickens before they hatch/c1 - wait for the results first.
